data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Simplified user interface to reduce cognitive load and decision time.
data.technical_spec: Minimalist design with essential options prominently displayed.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: UI elements designed for easy accessibility and interaction.
data.technical_spec: Key navigation elements are large and accessible within easy reach.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Focus on aesthetics to enhance perceived usability and facilitate trust.
data.technical_spec: Consistent visual design and use of Google's Material Design principles.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Adherence to the simplicity principle by limiting choices to prevent user overload.
data.technical_spec: Average of 5-9 options visible at a time, clearly categorized.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Interfaces are designed to meet user expectations based on common usage patterns.
data.technical_spec: Adoption of interfaces similar to common web expectations to reduce learning curve.
